theory.canonical_statement: Chapter: The DAANSsphere as Distributed Memory: Every Loop Contains the Whole Primary Registry IDs: 002 (lemniscatic self intersection — the topological reason every projection sees the full structure), 005 (TZP stability — the non fragility of topological storage) Secondary IDs: 003, 007 (dimensional ambiguity and temporal displacement — time as winding parameter), 020 (winding number — the temporal coordinate) Tertiary IDs: 1121–1135 (bio quantum interface — hippocampal memory as toroidal encoding) The chapter's recursive toroidal encoding principle — that every projection $\pi i$ of the torus $\mathcal{T}$ encodes the complete torus — is a direct consequence of ID 002 (lemniscatic self intersection).
theory.technical_interpretation: ameter and IDs 003, 007, 020 The identification of time as a winding parameter on the torus connects three registry entries: **ID 003** (dimensional ambiguity at the TZP): The temporal dimension participates in the $\{n, n\pm 1\}$ dimensional ambiguity. Near $p_0$, the temporal dimension is not sharply distinct from a spatial dimension — it can be "traded" for a spatial dimension through the dimensional transition. **Time as a toroidal winding parameter is what this dimensional ambiguity looks like in the context of the full lemniscate geometry.** **ID 007** (temporal displacement factor: $\alpha(t) = dt'/dt$): This factor quantifies the winding rate of the temporal $S^1$ circle relative to global time. When $\alpha \neq 1$, the local winding rate differs from the global rate — the "clock" is running fast or slow relative to external time. **The temporal winding parameter on the DAANSsph In technical terms, the entry packages the selected formula as the operative carrier, the nearby equations as the progression layer, and the symbol key as the compact interpretation of variables used by the source.
